**Claude** _2026-05-20_

1	---
     2	name: empower-v7
     3	version: 7.0.0
     4	description: |
     5	  Use SEMPRE quando o usuário quiser auditar seu uso do Claude, medir performance,
     6	  comparar práticas com o gold standard Anthropic, gerar personalização de conta,
     7	  ou entender como está usando IA no trabalho real.
     8	
     9	  Gatilhos explícitos: "empower", "meta-análise", "auditar conversas",
    10	  "como estou usando Claude", "minha performance com Claude",
    11	  "benchmark", "estou acima da média", "personalização de conta",
    12	  "questionário de uso", "skill performance", "analyze my conversations",
    13	  "audit my Claude usage", "am I above average", "gerar perfil",
    14	  "dashboard de uso", "meu nível com Claude"
    15	
    16	  NÃO usar para: análise de código, criação de documentos, pesquisas externas,
    17	  tarefas que não envolvam avaliação do próprio uso do Claude pelo usuário.
    18	
    19	  ICP: qualquer usuário Claude (Free/Pro/Max) que quer melhorar seu desempenho
    20	  com IA — consultores, pesquisadores, educadores, desenvolvedores, criadores.

    24	    - bash_tool

    33	---
    34	
    35	# EMPOWER V7 · AI Usage Validation & Growth Engine
    36	
    37	## QUANDO USAR
    38	
    39	### Gatilhos Explícitos
    40	- "empower" / "meta-análise" / "auditar conversas" / "questionário de uso"
    41	- "como estou usando Claude" / "minha performance" / "meu nível com Claude"
    42	- "benchmark" / "estou acima da média" / "am I above average"
    43	- "personalização de conta" / "gerar perfil de uso" / "dashboard de uso"
    44	- "analyze my conversations" / "audit my Claude usage"
    45	
    46	### Gatilhos Contextuais
    47	- Usuário enviou export de conversas Claude (.json/.zip/.csv)
    48	- Usuário quer saber se está "usando Claude certo"
    49	- Usuário quer gerar prompt de personalização para sua conta
    50	- Usuário quer comparar seu uso com boas práticas
    51	
    52	### NÃO Usar Para
    53	- Criação de documentos, código, pesquisas externas
    54	- Qualquer tarefa que não seja avaliação do próprio uso do Claude
    55	- Análise de uso de outras IAs (escopo: Claude apenas)
    56	
    57	---
    58	
    59	## WORKFLOW CANÔNICO
    60	
    61	```
    62	INPUT ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    63	  Export de conversas  →  MODE A (Conversation Audit)
    64	  Sem export           →  MODE B (Guided Questionnaire)
    65	  
    66	FASE 1 — DETECT MODE
    67	  ├─ Arquivo(s) enviado(s)?  →  MODE A
    68	  └─ Sem arquivo            →  MODE B → render questionnaire inline
    69	
    70	FASE 2 — INGEST & NORMALIZE  [MODE A only]
    71	  ├─ Parse export (JSON/JSONL/CSV/ZIP)
    72	  ├─ Normalize to canonical V7 schema
    73	  ├─ Redact PII before any analysis
    74	  └─ Cynefin-classify each interaction (batch LLM call)
    75	
    76	FASE 3 — SCORE (7 dimensions)
    77	  ├─ D1 Delegation      [AI Fluency]
    78	  ├─ D2 Description     [AI Fluency + 21 Anthropic GPs]
    79	  ├─ D3 Discernment     [AI Fluency]
    80	  ├─ D4 Diligence       [AI Fluency + AI Act literacy]
    81	  ├─ P1 Personalization [Claude feature stack]
    82	  ├─ W1 Workflow        [Empower maturity rubric]
    83	  └─ G1 Governance      [AI Act Article 4 literacy-aligned]
    84	
    85	FASE 4 — BENCHMARK (external anchor — MANDATORY)
    86	  ├─ Map user score to 5-level benchmark:
    87	  │   Novice (20–35%) → Developing (36–55%) → Proficient (56–70%)
    88	  │   Advanced (71–85%) → Expert (86–100%)
    89	  ├─ Compute percentile vs anchor population
    90	  └─ Identify "biggest gap to next level"
    91	
    92	FASE 5 — GENERATE INLINE DASHBOARD
    93	  ├─ Render interactive HTML/SVG artifact
    94	  ├─ Header metrics: Alignment Index, Level, Benchmark %, Top Gap
    95	  ├─ Radar chart: 7 dimensions vs benchmark level
    96	  ├─ WITH/WITHOUT comparison charts (per Cynefin domain)
    97	  ├─ Dimension cards with evidence + next action
    98	  └─ Personalization prescription at bottom
    99	
   100	FASE 6 — DELIVERABLES
   101	  ├─ E-001 Inline dashboard (HTML/SVG)  ← PRIMARY
   102	  ├─ E-002 Executive summary (Markdown)
   103	  ├─ E-003 Personalization prompt (profile + style + project)
   104	  ├─ E-004 Command library (XLSX — reutilizable workflows)
   105	  └─ E-005 Redaction log (CSV)
   106	
   107	FASE 7 — #META-REGENERATION
   108	  └─ Emit inline JSON v7.0 → BACKLOG.md append in Drive
   109	```
   110	
   111	---
   112	
   113	## SCORING SYSTEM
   114	
   115	### Dimensões e Pesos
   116	
   117	| ID | Dimensão | Framework | Max pts | Peso |
   118	|----|----------|-----------|---------|------|
   119	| D1 | Delegation | AI Fluency | 40 | 15% |
   120	| D2 | Description | AI Fluency + 21 GPs | 40 | 25% |
   121	| D3 | Discernment | AI Fluency | 40 | 20% |
   122	| D4 | Diligence | AI Fluency | 40 | 15% |
   123	| P1 | Personalization | Claude stack | 40 | 10% |
   124	| W1 | Workflow | Empower rubric | 40 | 10% |
   125	| G1 | Governance | AI Act literacy | 40 | 5% |
   126	
   127	**Alignment Index** = weighted average of 7 dimensions, normalized 0–100%
   128	
   129	### Benchmark Anchors (External — NOT internal comparison)
   130	
   131	| Level | Score | Profile description |
   132	|-------|-------|---------------------|
   133	| **Novice** | 20–35% | Conversational use, no skills, generic prompts |
   134	| **Developing** | 36–55% | Aware of practices, inconsistent application |
   135	| **Proficient** | 56–70% | Regular structure, some XML, basic workflow |
   136	| **Advanced** | 71–85% | Full skill suite, systematic prompting, measurable ROI |
   137	| **Expert** | 86–100% | All 7 dimensions optimized, complete personalization |
   138	
   139	Source: Anthropic AI Fluency course + prompting best practices docs
   140	
   141	---
   142	
   143	## MODE B — QUESTIONNAIRE STRUCTURE
   144	
   145	6 sections (like SkillForge multi-step form):
   146	
   147	1. **Identidade** — role, experience, primary use cases
   148	2. **Delegação** — what you ask Claude to do
   149	3. **Estrutura** — how you write prompts
   150	4. **Discernimento** — how you validate outputs
   151	5. **Personalização** — Claude features you use
   152	6. **Objetivos** — what you want to improve
   153	
   154	Each section generates inferred scores per dimension.
   155	After step 6 → generate full inline dashboard from inferred data.
   156	Confidence label: "inferred (Mode B)" in all outputs.
   157	
   158	---
   159	
   160	## REFERÊNCIAS
   161	
   162	Consultar progressivamente — não carregar tudo de uma vez:
   163	
   164	| Arquivo | Propósito | Quando ler |
   165	|---------|-----------|------------|
   166	| `references/anthropic-gold-standard-v1.md` | 21 GPs operacionalizados | Fase 3 (scoring D2) |
   167	| `references/ai-fluency-4d-matrix.md` | Rubrica D1–D4 | Fase 3 (scoring D1-D4) |
   168	| `references/benchmark-anchors.md` | Anchor levels + percentis | Fase 4 (benchmark) |
   169	| `references/personalization-stack.md` | Layers P1 | Fase 3 + Fase 6 (E-003) |
   170	| `references/privacy-redaction.md` | PII detection rules | Fase 2 (normalize) |
   171	| `templates/dashboard-template.html` | Layout dashboard inline | Fase 5 |
   172	| `templates/questionnaire-template.html` | Mode B form | Fase 1 (Mode B) |
   173	| `scripts/score_dimensions.py` | Scoring engine | Fase 3 |
   174	| `scripts/compute_benchmark.py` | External anchor calc | Fase 4 |
   175	
   176	---
   177	
   178	## LANGUAGE RULES
   179	
   180	- NUNCA usar: "AI Act compliant", "Anthropic-approved", "certified"
   181	- SEMPRE usar: "Anthropic-aligned", "AI Act Article 4 literacy-aligned"
   182	- Todos os claims com evidência citada (URL ou fonte)
   183	- Mode B outputs: marcar como "inferred — Mode B" (não "measured")
   184	- PII redactada antes de qualquer output compartilhável
   185

title\\\\tLetramento em IA, personaliza\\u00e7\\u00e3o profissional e Skills: um estudo sobre produtividade e redu\\u00e7\\u00e3o de retrabalho\\\\nAbstract\\\\tEste estudo investiga se a combina\\u00e7\\u00e3o de letramento em IA, personaliza\\u00e7\\u00e3o profissional de conta, uso de Skills/ SKILL.md, frameworks determin\\u00edsticos e conectores melhora a performance operacional de contas de IA. A hip\\u00f3tese \\u00e9 que contas com maior estrutura\\u00e7\\u00e3o de contexto, instru\\u00e7\\u00f5es persistentes, workflows reutiliz\\u00e1veis e integra\\u00e7\\u00e3o com ferramentas externas produzem mais entreg\\u00e1veis com menos retrabalho do que contas pouco personalizadas. O estudo usa conversas hist\\u00f3ricas, exporta\\u00e7\\u00e3o de dados, an\\u00e1lise comparativa entre contas e m\\u00e9tricas de tempo, intera\\u00e7\\u00e3o e entrega documental para construir um ranking de efici\\u00eancia. O resultado esperado \\u00e9 um framework replic\\u00e1vel para onboarding, personaliza\\u00e7\\u00e3o e expans\\u00e3o multicanal de conte\\u00fado.\\\\nBroad search command\\\\tSearch for the closest academic and practical benchmarks to: AI literacy, prompt engineering, AI account personalization, SKILL.md / skills, deterministic frameworks, connectors, productivity, rework reduction, cross-platform content repurposing, and onboarding systems. Include US and Brazil. Prefer peer-reviewed papers, official documentation, repositories, and benchmark studies. Return the closest matches, summarize methods, report similarity by dimension (topic, method, tooling, outcome), and separate evidence from inference.\\\\nFundamental research question\\\\tDoes structured AI account design measurably improve deliverable output and reduce rework compared with unstructured usage?\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: Questions\\\\n\\\\nCore questions\\\\n1. Which published work is conceptually closest to this thesis?\\\\n2. Which benchmarks exist in the US and Brazil for AI literacy, prompt engineering, and workflow personalization?\\\\n3. What operating model best explains gains from personalization, skills, and connectors?\\\\n4. How should ROI be measured when comparing accounts with different histories and purposes?\\\\n5. Which quality controls are needed before claiming that higher volume means better output?\\\\n6. What minimum evidence is needed to publish this as a credible benchmark?\\\\n7. How can the framework be repurposed into blog, Medium, GitHub, LinkedIn, X, and YouTube formats?\\\\n8. What should be standardized in the onboarding so the system remains reproducible?\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: Source Log\\\\n\\\\nID\\\\tQuery / Search term\\\\tSource type\\\\tTitle\\\\tAuthor / \"toolu_01KUgWpmVqcswKHih3ZCex57\", \"name\": \"bash_tool\", \"content\": [{\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"{\\\"returncode\\\":0,\\\"stdout\\\":\\\"=== AIGOVBRASIL V3 EXEC XLSX ===\\\\n## Sheet: 01_CMD\\\\n\\\\nAI Gov Brasil \\u2014 CMD V1\\\\nCONTEXT\\\\nLeonardo Batista is building AI Gov Brasil \\u2014 bilingual (PT-BR + EN) editorial and operational brand for AI governance and AI literacy for non-developer Brazilian knowledge workers. The 90-day plan and all decisions are in this Traveller DB.\\\\nCONFIRMED DECISIONS (2026-05-14)\\\\n\\u2022 D-A: S\\u00e3o Paulo Claude community/event track \\u2192 YES\\\\n\\u2022 D-B: Bilingual EN + PT-BR \\u2192 CONFIRMED\\\\n\\u2022 D-C: Paid newsletter tier (Beehiiv recommended) \\u2192 CONFIRMED\\\\nACTIVE 90-DAY PHASE\\\\n[To be updated each session: Setup / Launch / Validation / Monetization Signal]\\\\nOPEN DECISIONS PENDING LEONARDO\\\\nSee DECISIONS sheet \\u2014 D-001 through D-010 not yet confirmed.\\\\nNEXT-SESSION TASKS\\\\n1. Verify newsletter platform decision (Beehiiv vs alternatives)\\\\n2. Verify Cubo Ita\\u00fa venue conversation status\\\\n3. Verify INPI trademark search completion\\\\n4. Status-check 90-day metrics against roadmap targets\\\\n5. Update CHANGELOG with session deliverables\\\\nEDITORIAL STANDARD\\\\nBBC + HBR + AP. Bilingual. Evidence-led. No hype. Every quantitative claim sourced or labeled [inference]. EU AI Act Article 4 framing. Operational literacy, not compliance guidance.\\\\nDO NOT\\\\n\\u2022 Claim LinkedIn Top Voice can be applied for (it cannot)\\\\n\\u2022 Present any ROI figure without [inference] label or named source\\\\n\\u2022 Recommend more than three monetization streams for 90 days\\\\n\\u2022 Use 'AI will replace' framing\\\\nCLOSING RULE\\\\nEnd every session with one concrete next action, not a menu.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 02_STRATEGIC_READ\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tAssessment\\\\nWhat is strong\\\\tEditorial discipline (BBC + HBR + AP), verified evidence stack (M-007 through M-013, AI-FLU, EU-ACT Article 4), 24-skill operational corpus, non-developer ICP focus rare in Brazilian AI content market. Operator depth + editorial sobriety = moat.\\\\nWhat needs reframing\\\\tAnthropic ambassador goal framed as 'build portfolio, then apply.' Claude Community Ambassadors Program is live (March 2026), explicitly event-focused. Without recurring Brazilian Claude community surface, application underperforms. D-A (S\\u00e3o Paulo community track) is foundational.\\\\nWhat web search changed\\\\t(1) Ambassador path is live, global, event-based, accepts non-developers with Claude Code/Cowork experience + community involvement. (2) LinkedIn Top Voice is invitation-only, ~300/year, 6-12 month build. (3) GitHub AI-governance dominated by curated awesome-lists; curatorial differentiation closed, operational differentiation (Claude Skills + PT-BR + non-dev) wide open.\\\\nThree Most Important Decisions Resolved\\\\t\\\\nD-A: Brazilian Claude community/event track\\\\tCONFIRMED by Leonardo 2026-05-14\\\\nD-B: LinkedIn primary language\\\\tBilingual EN + PT-BR, CONFIRMED\\\\nD-C: First monetization to test\\\\tPaid newsletter tier, CONFIRMED\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 03_BRAND_IDENTITY\\\\n\\\\nQuestion\\\\tLabel\\\\tValue\\\\nQ-001: Name\\\\tconfirmed\\\\tAI Gov Brasil\\\\nQ-002: Domain\\\\tconfirmed\\\\taigovbrasil.com\\\\nQ-003: Handle\\\\tconfirmed\\\\t@aigovbrasil\\\\nQ-004: Brand Type\\\\tinference\\\\tHybrid \\u2014 editorial publication (Review Journal) + practitioner consultancy (operator/skills) + community surface (meetup/ambassador). Hybrid is intentional: editorial credibility funds practitioner credibility funds community credibility.\\\\nQ-005: One-Sentence\\\\tinference\\\\tAI Gov Brasil is the Brazilian operating system for AI governance and AI literacy for knowledge workers who use AI at work.\\\\nQ-007: Mission\\\\tinference\\\\tTo raise the operational AI literacy of Brazilian knowledge workers from passive consumption to fluent, governed, evidence-led use.\\\\nQ-008: Vision\\\\tinference\\\\tBrazil becomes a recognized global voice in operational AI literacy \\u2014 not by adopting AI fastest, but by operating it most responsibly.\\\\nQ-009: Values\\\\tinference\\\\tEvidence over hype. Operator depth over influencer reach. Sober editorial discipline over performative expertise. Open artifacts over gated guru content. Bilingual access over English-only gatekeeping.\\\\nQ-010: Thesis\\\\tconfirmed\\\\tThe next competitive advantage is not using AI first. It is operating AI with fluency.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 04_POSITIONING\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tContent\\\\nKnown For\\\\tOperational AI literacy and Claude workflows for non-developers, with EU AI Act and Brazilian regulatory framing.\\\\nCategory\\\\tAI Governance \\u00d7 AI Literacy \\u00d7 Workflow Design \\u2014 category currently fragmented between regulatory consultants (top-down) and prompt influencers (bottom-up). AI Gov Brasil occupies the operator middle.\\\\nProblem Solved\\\\tKnowledge workers use AI daily but lack governance discipline. KPMG + Melbourne (48,340 people): 56% have made errors at work because of AI; 66% use AI outputs without verification. Workday/Hanover: ~40% of time saved by AI is lost to rework. The gap is not access \\u2014 it is method.\\\\nFor Whom (ICP)\\\\tICP-002: non-developer knowledge workers \\u2014 analysts, consultants, founders, educators, operators, writers, executives \\u2014 who use Claude/ChatGPT daily for real work.\\\\nPromise\\\\tReliable AI output, fewer rework hours, traceable workflows, and professional credibility with AI tools \\u2014 without learning to code.\\\\nNever Promise\\\\tNever promise '10x productivity,' 'replace your team,' 'AI will write your job,' specific revenue figure from AI use, or any framing that positions AI as substitute for human judgment. Never claim to give legal compliance advice.\\\\nDifferentiator\\\\tOperator-first, not influencer-first. Evidence-led, not hype-led. Bilingual PT-BR/EN. Anchored to verified primary sources (KPMG, Workday, Anthropic AI Fluency Index, EU AI Act Article 4). Publishes open skills and case studies rather than gated courses.\\\\nEnemy\\\\tGeneric AI content, productivity hype, '10 prompts to get rich' guru culture, irresponsible AI use, low AI literacy framed as expertise.\\\\nPositioning Statement\\\\tFor Brazilian knowledge workers who use AI at work and want professional credibility with it, AI Gov Brasil is the operational AI governance and literacy brand that publishes evidence-led workflows, Claude Skills, and case studies \\u2014 because operating AI with fluency, not adopting it first, is the next competitive advantage.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 05_ICP\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tContent\\\\nPrimary Audience\\\\tICP-002: Brazilian non-developer knowledge workers using AI at work \\u2014 analysts, consultants, managers, founders, educators, writers, operators.\\\\nSecondary Audience\\\\tBrazilian HR/L&D leaders responsible for AI literacy programs (EU AI Act Article 4 compliance), and Anthropic-aligned community participants in S\\u00e3o Paulo and broader Brazil.\\\\nTechnical Level\\\\tNon-technical to lightly technical. Comfortable with Claude.ai, ChatGPT, Notion, Google Workspace. Not comfortable with APIs, terminals, or code.\\\\nRoles\\\\tStrategy consultants, product managers, marketing analysts, founders of <50-person companies, educators in higher ed and corporate training, content writers and editors, internal operations leads.\\\\nSectors\\\\tProfessional services, education, media and publishing, consulting, startups, corporate operations/L&D departments \\u2014 particularly Brazilian firms with EU clients or EU exposure.\\\\nPrimary Pain\\\\tTime lost to AI rework (Workday: ~40% of AI time savings lost to correction). Hidden AI use at work (Microsoft Work Trend Index: 52% hide AI use, 53% fear appearing replaceable). Inability to defend AI-assisted output professionally.\\\\nJobs to be Done\\\\tProduce reliable AI output without rework. Defend AI-assisted work to skeptical managers. Build traceable, reusable workflow. Demonstrate AI literacy as professional skill. Comply with emerging regulation (AI Act Article 4 for EU-exposed firms; Brazilian PL 2338 when enacted).\\\\nWhere They Are\\\\tLinkedIn (primary professional surface for Brazilian non-dev knowledge workers), Medium (long-form), Twitter/X (lighter touch), Newsletter email (deeper relationship), in-person SP meetups (highest-trust surface).\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 06_DECISIONS\\\\n\\\\nID\\\\tDecision\\\\tOptions\\\\tRecommendation\\\\tStatus\\\\nD-001\\\\tNewsletter platform choice\\\\tBeehiiv / Substack / Ghost / ConvertKit\\\\tBeehiiv\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-002\\\\tSP meetup format\\\\tOnline-only / Hybrid SP / In-person SP\\\\tHybrid month 1, in-person from month 2\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-003\\\\tVenue partner for SP meetups\\\\tCubo Ita\\u00fa / WeWork / University / Independent\\\\tCubo Ita\\u00fa + university parallel\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-004\\\\tRepository license\\\\tMIT / Apache 2.0 / CC BY 4.0 for docs\\\\tMIT code + CC BY 4.0 docs\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-005\\\\tEU AI Act content positioning\\\\tDisclaimer / 'Operational literacy' / Both\\\\tBoth\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-006\\\\tBrazilian academic co-signer\\\\tYes recruit / No solo / Optional organic\\\\tOptional now, active month 4-6\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-007\\\\tAI Gov Brasil trademark (INPI)\\\\tYes now / Wait until monetization\\\\tSearch now, file day 60 if clear\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-008\\\\tAmbassador application timing\\\\tDay 45 / Day 75-90 / After month 4\\\\tDay 75-90\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-009\\\\tBrazilian AI gov thesis document\\\\tYes day 30 / No distributed\\\\tYes day 30, bilingual\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-010\\\\tBilingual content ratio\\\\t50/50 / 60% PT-BR / 60% EN\\\\t55% PT-BR / 45% EN\\\\tPENDING\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 07_ROADMAP_90D\\\\n\\\\nPhase\\\\tNarrative\\\\tKey Metrics\\\\nPhase 1 Setup Days 1-14\\\\tFoundation laid. Repository at v1.0.0 with all gaps closed. LinkedIn profile gold-standard, bilingual, with first 10 posts published. Claude Projects configured across five workspaces. Newsletter platform chosen and live. First meetup announced for day 30 with venue confirmed.\\\\t\\u2022 Repository v1.0.0 tag pushed \\u2022 10 LinkedIn posts, avg >500 impressions \\u2022 Newsletter at 100+ free subscribers\\\\nPhase 2 Launch Days 15-45\\\\tFirst content wave shipped. D2 published on Medium. D3 published on GitHub. D1 distributed via newsletter. First S\\u00e3o Paulo Claude meetup hosted (day 30, online or hybrid) with public recap and case study. LinkedIn cadence sustained at 3 posts/week. First inbound from ICP-002 unprompted.\\\\t\\u2022 First meetup: 20+ attendees, public recap \\u2022 LinkedIn at 2,000+ followers \\u2022 Newsletter at 500+ free subscribers\\\\nPhase 3 Validation Days 46-75\\\\tSignal differentiated from noise. Top-performing content pillar identified and doubled. Second meetup hosted (day 60). Second Medium long-form published. Ambassador application materials assembled. Three credible Anthropic-adjacent engagers visibly active on LinkedIn.\\\\t\\u2022 LinkedIn at 3,500+ followers, avg >3k impressions \\u2022 Newsletter at 800+ free subscribers \\u2022 Two meetups hosted, public recaps\\\\nPhase 4 Monetization Days 76-90\\\\tPaid newsletter tier launches at day 80. Anthropic Community Ambassador application submitted at day 80-85. Third meetup hosted (day 90). Decision point: paid tier conversion rate above or below 3% determines whether newsletter is primary monetization stream.\\\\t\\u2022 Paid tier: \\u226530 paying subscribers within 14 days \\u2022 Ambassador application submitted \\u2022 LinkedIn at 5,000+ followers\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 08_RISKS\\\\n\\\\nID\\\\tRisk\\\\tProbability\\\\tImpact\\\\tMitigation\\\\nR-001\\\\tEU AI Act content read as legal advice\\\\tMedium\\\\tHigh\\\\tExplicit non-legal-advice disclaimer in repo root and at bottom of every regulatory post. 'Operational literacy' framing throughout. Never use 'compliance guidance' or 'legal advice' in public content.\\\\nR-002\\\\tBrand collision \\u2014 'AI Gov Brasil' overlaps with existing Brazilian government or academic initiative\\\\tLow-Medium\\\\tHigh\\\\tINPI prior-art search at day 1; GitHub org search; Google search before public launch.\\\\nR-003\\\\tAnthropic ambassador application rejected due to lack of event evidence\\\\tLow with D-A\\\\tHigh\\\\tExecute SP Claude meetup track from day 15 onward; ensure at least three documented meetups before applying.\\\\nR-004\\\\tBilingual workload exceeds sustainable capacity\\\\tMedium-High\\\\tMedium\\\\tShared evidence library (Dados.md) as single source; 70% parallel / 30% native; CMD template; week-6 review with permission to drop to one language if needed.\\\\nR-005\\\\tPaid newsletter launches with too few free subscribers\\\\tMedium\\\\tMedium\\\\tDo not launch paid tier until free subscriber count exceeds 1,000. If day-80 < 1,000, delay to days 90-100.\\\\nR-006\\\\tTrust & Safety / Acceptable Use Policy violation\\\\tLow-Medium\\\\tVery High\\\\tBefore any post mentioning Claude capabilities, verify against current Anthropic Usage Policies. Use precise language. Never present hypothetical use cases as endorsed.\\\\nR-007\\\\tSingle-operator burnout\\\\tHigh\\\\tMedium-High\\\\tCapacity audit end of week 4. Explicit permission to drop to two posts/week if signals support. Use ADHD desk dashboard skill.\\\\nR-008\\\\tGitHub awesome-list crowd-out\\\\tMedium\\\\tMedium\\\\tRoot README opens with 'This is not an awesome-list. This is an operational corpus.' Differentiation stated top-of-fold.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 09_LINKEDIN_STRATEGY\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tContent\\\\nTop Voice Reality Check\\\\tBlue Top Voice badge is invitation-only, awarded by LinkedIn's editorial team to ~300 global experts per year, reviewed twice yearly, valid for at least 6 months. Gold Community Top Voice badge was discontinued Oct 8, 2024. Realistic build window: 6-12 months of consistent original posting in defined niche with engagement quality, not follower count, as primary signal.\\\\n90-Day Framing\\\\t90 days does not earn the badge. 90 days earns the foundation that earns the invitation in months 6-12: defined niche (Brazilian non-dev AI governance), consistent cadence (3 posts/week minimum), original perspectives (not aggregation), network of credible engagers \\u2014 Anthropic employees, AI governance researchers in Brazil, Brazilian non-dev AI practitioners.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nContent Pillars\\\\t\\\\nPillar 1: Evidence\\\\tWeekly post anchored to primary source (KPMG, Workday, Anthropic, EU AI Act). Format: data point \\u2192 editorial reading \\u2192 operational implication.\\\\nPillar 2: Skill of Week\\\\tWalk through one Claude Skill from 24-skill corpus, with actual prompt/configuration and non-dev use case.\\\\nPillar 3: Case Study\\\\tReproducible workflow with before/after, time saved, evidence of error reduction. Review Journal voice.\\\\nPillar 4: Brazilian Regulatory\\\\tPL 2338, LGPD intersections with AI, EU AI Act Article 4 obligations for Brazilian firms with EU clients.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nCadence & Format\\\\t\\\\nWeekly Cadence\\\\t3 posts per week minimum. Mon: PT-BR operational tip (150-250 words). Wed: EN evidence-led analysis (250-400 words). Fri: bilingual case study or Brazilian regulatory. Two long-form LinkedIn articles per month.\\\\nDaily Engagement\\\\t30 minutes per day commenting substantively on 5-10 posts from people in target network.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nFirst 50 Followers Curation\\\\t\\\\nStrategy\\\\tEditorial team sees who engages with your posts. In days 1-30, deliberately curate first 50 connections: Anthropic employees publicly active on LinkedIn, AI governance researchers at Brazilian universities (USP, FGV, Insper), Brazilian non-dev AI creators with editorial sobriety, EU AI Act practitioners. Quality of network beats follower count for editorial visibility.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nKPI Targets\\\\t\\\\nDay 30\\\\t1,000 followers, 3 weekly posts maintained, average engagement rate >2%, 25 substantive comments delivered to target network per week.\\\\nDay 60\\\\t2,500 followers, 1 long-form article with >10k impressions, first inbound from ICP-002 unprompted.\\\\nDay 90\\\\t5,000 followers, average post >5k impressions, 3+ Anthropic-adjacent engagers commenting on posts, foundation visible for editorial invitation in months 4-9.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 10_AMBASSADOR_PATH\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tContent\\\\nProgram Status\\\\tClaude Community Ambassadors Program is open as of March 2026 at claude.com/community/ambassadors. Applications reviewed on rolling basis. Program prioritizes geographic diversity and explicitly welcomes international applicants. No developer title requirement. Benefits include event funding, monthly API credits, private Slack with Anthropic team, Builders Council access, swag, and pre-release feature access. Program does not pay directly. Ambassadors cannot also represent company whose core business competes with Anthropic's.\\\\nLeonardo Fit Assessment\\\\tStrong fit on three dimensions, weak on one. Strong: editorial credibility (Review Journal, BBC+HBR+AP), operational depth (24 Claude Skills), geographic diversity (Brazil, S\\u00e3o Paulo, currently under-represented). Weak: community/event track record currently absent. D-A (S\\u00e3o Paulo Claude meetups) directly closes this gap. Without it, application leans on content credibility alone, which is not program's selection signal.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nAsset Checklist Before Application\\\\t\\\\n\\u2713 Repository\\\\tPublic repository live with v1.0.0 release tag\\\\n\\u2713 LinkedIn\\\\tProfile gold-standard with 3,000+ followers and consistent posting record (>30 days)\\\\n\\u2713 First Meetup\\\\tClaude meetup hosted in S\\u00e3o Paulo (online or hybrid acceptable) with public recap\\\\n\\u2713 Medium\\\\tAt least one Medium long-form (D2) published\\\\n\\u2713 GitHub Case\\\\tCase study (D3) published on GitHub\\\\n\\u2713 Claude Product\\\\tDocumented experience with Claude Code OR Claude Cowork \\u2014 publish at least one workflow case study using one of these two products\\\\n\\u2713 Landing\\\\tBilingual README on aigovbrasil.com landing\\\\n\\\",\\\"stderr\\\":\\\"\\\"}\", \"uuid\": \"aa77ac65-07f7-4776-aa43-d7f6e0104482\"}], \"is_error\": false, \"structured_content\": null, \"meta\": null, \"message\": null, \"integration_name\": null, \"mcp_server_url\": null, \"integration_icon_url\": null, \"icon_name\": \"commandLine\", \"display_content\": {\"type\": \"json_block\", \"json_block\": \"{\\\"returncode\\\":0,\\\"stdout\\\":\\\"=== AIGOVBRASIL V3 EXEC XLSX ===\\\\n## Sheet: 01_CMD\\\\n\\\\nAI Gov Brasil \\u2014 CMD V1\\\\nCONTEXT\\\\nLeonardo Batista is building AI Gov Brasil \\u2014 bilingual (PT-BR + EN) editorial and operational brand for AI governance and AI literacy for non-developer Brazilian knowledge workers. The 90-day plan and all decisions are in this Traveller DB.\\\\nCONFIRMED DECISIONS (2026-05-14)\\\\n\\u2022 D-A: S\\u00e3o Paulo Claude community/event track \\u2192 YES\\\\n\\u2022 D-B: Bilingual EN + PT-BR \\u2192 CONFIRMED\\\\n\\u2022 D-C: Paid newsletter tier (Beehiiv recommended) \\u2192 CONFIRMED\\\\nACTIVE 90-DAY PHASE\\\\n[To be updated each session: Setup / Launch / Validation / Monetization Signal]\\\\nOPEN DECISIONS PENDING LEONARDO\\\\nSee DECISIONS sheet \\u2014 D-001 through D-010 not yet confirmed.\\\\nNEXT-SESSION TASKS\\\\n1. Verify newsletter platform decision (Beehiiv vs alternatives)\\\\n2. Verify Cubo Ita\\u00fa venue conversation status\\\\n3. Verify INPI trademark search completion\\\\n4. Status-check 90-day metrics against roadmap targets\\\\n5. Update CHANGELOG with session deliverables\\\\nEDITORIAL STANDARD\\\\nBBC + HBR + AP. Bilingual. Evidence-led. No hype. Every quantitative claim sourced or labeled [inference]. EU AI Act Article 4 framing. Operational literacy, not compliance guidance.\\\\nDO NOT\\\\n\\u2022 Claim LinkedIn Top Voice can be applied for (it cannot)\\\\n\\u2022 Present any ROI figure without [inference] label or named source\\\\n\\u2022 Recommend more than three monetization streams for 90 days\\\\n\\u2022 Use 'AI will replace' framing\\\\nCLOSING RULE\\\\nEnd every session with one concrete next action, not a menu.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 02_STRATEGIC_READ\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tAssessment\\\\nWhat is strong\\\\tEditorial discipline (BBC + HBR + AP), verified evidence stack (M-007 through M-013, AI-FLU, EU-ACT Article 4), 24-skill operational corpus, non-developer ICP focus rare in Brazilian AI content market. Operator depth + editorial sobriety = moat.\\\\nWhat needs reframing\\\\tAnthropic ambassador goal framed as 'build portfolio, then apply.' Claude Community Ambassadors Program is live (March 2026), explicitly event-focused. Without recurring Brazilian Claude community surface, application underperforms. D-A (S\\u00e3o Paulo community track) is foundational.\\\\nWhat web search changed\\\\t(1) Ambassador path is live, global, event-based, accepts non-developers with Claude Code/Cowork experience + community involvement. (2) LinkedIn Top Voice is invitation-only, ~300/year, 6-12 month build. (3) GitHub AI-governance dominated by curated awesome-lists; curatorial differentiation closed, operational differentiation (Claude Skills + PT-BR + non-dev) wide open.\\\\nThree Most Important Decisions Resolved\\\\t\\\\nD-A: Brazilian Claude community/event track\\\\tCONFIRMED by Leonardo 2026-05-14\\\\nD-B: LinkedIn primary language\\\\tBilingual EN + PT-BR, CONFIRMED\\\\nD-C: First monetization to test\\\\tPaid newsletter tier, CONFIRMED\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 03_BRAND_IDENTITY\\\\n\\\\nQuestion\\\\tLabel\\\\tValue\\\\nQ-001: Name\\\\tconfirmed\\\\tAI Gov Brasil\\\\nQ-002: Domain\\\\tconfirmed\\\\taigovbrasil.com\\\\nQ-003: Handle\\\\tconfirmed\\\\t@aigovbrasil\\\\nQ-004: Brand Type\\\\tinference\\\\tHybrid \\u2014 editorial publication (Review Journal) + practitioner consultancy (operator/skills) + community surface (meetup/ambassador). Hybrid is intentional: editorial credibility funds practitioner credibility funds community credibility.\\\\nQ-005: One-Sentence\\\\tinference\\\\tAI Gov Brasil is the Brazilian operating system for AI governance and AI literacy for knowledge workers who use AI at work.\\\\nQ-007: Mission\\\\tinference\\\\tTo raise the operational AI literacy of Brazilian knowledge workers from passive consumption to fluent, governed, evidence-led use.\\\\nQ-008: Vision\\\\tinference\\\\tBrazil becomes a recognized global voice in operational AI literacy \\u2014 not by adopting AI fastest, but by operating it most responsibly.\\\\nQ-009: Values\\\\tinference\\\\tEvidence over hype. Operator depth over influencer reach. Sober editorial discipline over performative expertise. Open artifacts over gated guru content. Bilingual access over English-only gatekeeping.\\\\nQ-010: Thesis\\\\tconfirmed\\\\tThe next competitive advantage is not using AI first. It is operating AI with fluency.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 04_POSITIONING\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tContent\\\\nKnown For\\\\tOperational AI literacy and Claude workflows for non-developers, with EU AI Act and Brazilian regulatory framing.\\\\nCategory\\\\tAI Governance \\u00d7 AI Literacy \\u00d7 Workflow Design \\u2014 category currently fragmented between regulatory consultants (top-down) and prompt influencers (bottom-up). AI Gov Brasil occupies the operator middle.\\\\nProblem Solved\\\\tKnowledge workers use AI daily but lack governance discipline. KPMG + Melbourne (48,340 people): 56% have made errors at work because of AI; 66% use AI outputs without verification. Workday/Hanover: ~40% of time saved by AI is lost to rework. The gap is not access \\u2014 it is method.\\\\nFor Whom (ICP)\\\\tICP-002: non-developer knowledge workers \\u2014 analysts, consultants, founders, educators, operators, writers, executives \\u2014 who use Claude/ChatGPT daily for real work.\\\\nPromise\\\\tReliable AI output, fewer rework hours, traceable workflows, and professional credibility with AI tools \\u2014 without learning to code.\\\\nNever Promise\\\\tNever promise '10x productivity,' 'replace your team,' 'AI will write your job,' specific revenue figure from AI use, or any framing that positions AI as substitute for human judgment. Never claim to give legal compliance advice.\\\\nDifferentiator\\\\tOperator-first, not influencer-first. Evidence-led, not hype-led. Bilingual PT-BR/EN. Anchored to verified primary sources (KPMG, Workday, Anthropic AI Fluency Index, EU AI Act Article 4). Publishes open skills and case studies rather than gated courses.\\\\nEnemy\\\\tGeneric AI content, productivity hype, '10 prompts to get rich' guru culture, irresponsible AI use, low AI literacy framed as expertise.\\\\nPositioning Statement\\\\tFor Brazilian knowledge workers who use AI at work and want professional credibility with it, AI Gov Brasil is the operational AI governance and literacy brand that publishes evidence-led workflows, Claude Skills, and case studies \\u2014 because operating AI with fluency, not adopting it first, is the next competitive advantage.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 05_ICP\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tContent\\\\nPrimary Audience\\\\tICP-002: Brazilian non-developer knowledge workers using AI at work \\u2014 analysts, consultants, managers, founders, educators, writers, operators.\\\\nSecondary Audience\\\\tBrazilian HR/L&D leaders responsible for AI literacy programs (EU AI Act Article 4 compliance), and Anthropic-aligned community participants in S\\u00e3o Paulo and broader Brazil.\\\\nTechnical Level\\\\tNon-technical to lightly technical. Comfortable with Claude.ai, ChatGPT, Notion, Google Workspace. Not comfortable with APIs, terminals, or code.\\\\nRoles\\\\tStrategy consultants, product managers, marketing analysts, founders of <50-person companies, educators in higher ed and corporate training, content writers and editors, internal operations leads.\\\\nSectors\\\\tProfessional services, education, media and publishing, consulting, startups, corporate operations/L&D departments \\u2014 particularly Brazilian firms with EU clients or EU exposure.\\\\nPrimary Pain\\\\tTime lost to AI rework (Workday: ~40% of AI time savings lost to correction). Hidden AI use at work (Microsoft Work Trend Index: 52% hide AI use, 53% fear appearing replaceable). Inability to defend AI-assisted output professionally.\\\\nJobs to be Done\\\\tProduce reliable AI output without rework. Defend AI-assisted work to skeptical managers. Build traceable, reusable workflow. Demonstrate AI literacy as professional skill. Comply with emerging regulation (AI Act Article 4 for EU-exposed firms; Brazilian PL 2338 when enacted).\\\\nWhere They Are\\\\tLinkedIn (primary professional surface for Brazilian non-dev knowledge workers), Medium (long-form), Twitter/X (lighter touch), Newsletter email (deeper relationship), in-person SP meetups (highest-trust surface).\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 06_DECISIONS\\\\n\\\\nID\\\\tDecision\\\\tOptions\\\\tRecommendation\\\\tStatus\\\\nD-001\\\\tNewsletter platform choice\\\\tBeehiiv / Substack / Ghost / ConvertKit\\\\tBeehiiv\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-002\\\\tSP meetup format\\\\tOnline-only / Hybrid SP / In-person SP\\\\tHybrid month 1, in-person from month 2\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-003\\\\tVenue partner for SP meetups\\\\tCubo Ita\\u00fa / WeWork / University / Independent\\\\tCubo Ita\\u00fa + university parallel\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-004\\\\tRepository license\\\\tMIT / Apache 2.0 / CC BY 4.0 for docs\\\\tMIT code + CC BY 4.0 docs\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-005\\\\tEU AI Act content positioning\\\\tDisclaimer / 'Operational literacy' / Both\\\\tBoth\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-006\\\\tBrazilian academic co-signer\\\\tYes recruit / No solo / Optional organic\\\\tOptional now, active month 4-6\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-007\\\\tAI Gov Brasil trademark (INPI)\\\\tYes now / Wait until monetization\\\\tSearch now, file day 60 if clear\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-008\\\\tAmbassador application timing\\\\tDay 45 / Day 75-90 / After month 4\\\\tDay 75-90\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-009\\\\tBrazilian AI gov thesis document\\\\tYes day 30 / No distributed\\\\tYes day 30, bilingual\\\\tPENDING\\\\nD-010\\\\tBilingual content ratio\\\\t50/50 / 60% PT-BR / 60% EN\\\\t55% PT-BR / 45% EN\\\\tPENDING\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 07_ROADMAP_90D\\\\n\\\\nPhase\\\\tNarrative\\\\tKey Metrics\\\\nPhase 1 Setup Days 1-14\\\\tFoundation laid. Repository at v1.0.0 with all gaps closed. LinkedIn profile gold-standard, bilingual, with first 10 posts published. Claude Projects configured across five workspaces. Newsletter platform chosen and live. First meetup announced for day 30 with venue confirmed.\\\\t\\u2022 Repository v1.0.0 tag pushed \\u2022 10 LinkedIn posts, avg >500 impressions \\u2022 Newsletter at 100+ free subscribers\\\\nPhase 2 Launch Days 15-45\\\\tFirst content wave shipped. D2 published on Medium. D3 published on GitHub. D1 distributed via newsletter. First S\\u00e3o Paulo Claude meetup hosted (day 30, online or hybrid) with public recap and case study. LinkedIn cadence sustained at 3 posts/week. First inbound from ICP-002 unprompted.\\\\t\\u2022 First meetup: 20+ attendees, public recap \\u2022 LinkedIn at 2,000+ followers \\u2022 Newsletter at 500+ free subscribers\\\\nPhase 3 Validation Days 46-75\\\\tSignal differentiated from noise. Top-performing content pillar identified and doubled. Second meetup hosted (day 60). Second Medium long-form published. Ambassador application materials assembled. Three credible Anthropic-adjacent engagers visibly active on LinkedIn.\\\\t\\u2022 LinkedIn at 3,500+ followers, avg >3k impressions \\u2022 Newsletter at 800+ free subscribers \\u2022 Two meetups hosted, public recaps\\\\nPhase 4 Monetization Days 76-90\\\\tPaid newsletter tier launches at day 80. Anthropic Community Ambassador application submitted at day 80-85. Third meetup hosted (day 90). Decision point: paid tier conversion rate above or below 3% determines whether newsletter is primary monetization stream.\\\\t\\u2022 Paid tier: \\u226530 paying subscribers within 14 days \\u2022 Ambassador application submitted \\u2022 LinkedIn at 5,000+ followers\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 08_RISKS\\\\n\\\\nID\\\\tRisk\\\\tProbability\\\\tImpact\\\\tMitigation\\\\nR-001\\\\tEU AI Act content read as legal advice\\\\tMedium\\\\tHigh\\\\tExplicit non-legal-advice disclaimer in repo root and at bottom of every regulatory post. 'Operational literacy' framing throughout. Never use 'compliance guidance' or 'legal advice' in public content.\\\\nR-002\\\\tBrand collision \\u2014 'AI Gov Brasil' overlaps with existing Brazilian government or academic initiative\\\\tLow-Medium\\\\tHigh\\\\tINPI prior-art search at day 1; GitHub org search; Google search before public launch.\\\\nR-003\\\\tAnthropic ambassador application rejected due to lack of event evidence\\\\tLow with D-A\\\\tHigh\\\\tExecute SP Claude meetup track from day 15 onward; ensure at least three documented meetups before applying.\\\\nR-004\\\\tBilingual workload exceeds sustainable capacity\\\\tMedium-High\\\\tMedium\\\\tShared evidence library (Dados.md) as single source; 70% parallel / 30% native; CMD template; week-6 review with permission to drop to one language if needed.\\\\nR-005\\\\tPaid newsletter launches with too few free subscribers\\\\tMedium\\\\tMedium\\\\tDo not launch paid tier until free subscriber count exceeds 1,000. If day-80 < 1,000, delay to days 90-100.\\\\nR-006\\\\tTrust & Safety / Acceptable Use Policy violation\\\\tLow-Medium\\\\tVery High\\\\tBefore any post mentioning Claude capabilities, verify against current Anthropic Usage Policies. Use precise language. Never present hypothetical use cases as endorsed.\\\\nR-007\\\\tSingle-operator burnout\\\\tHigh\\\\tMedium-High\\\\tCapacity audit end of week 4. Explicit permission to drop to two posts/week if signals support. Use ADHD desk dashboard skill.\\\\nR-008\\\\tGitHub awesome-list crowd-out\\\\tMedium\\\\tMedium\\\\tRoot README opens with 'This is not an awesome-list. This is an operational corpus.' Differentiation stated top-of-fold.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 09_LINKEDIN_STRATEGY\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tContent\\\\nTop Voice Reality Check\\\\tBlue Top Voice badge is invitation-only, awarded by LinkedIn's editorial team to ~300 global experts per year, reviewed twice yearly, valid for at least 6 months. Gold Community Top Voice badge was discontinued Oct 8, 2024. Realistic build window: 6-12 months of consistent original posting in defined niche with engagement quality, not follower count, as primary signal.\\\\n90-Day Framing\\\\t90 days does not earn the badge. 90 days earns the foundation that earns the invitation in months 6-12: defined niche (Brazilian non-dev AI governance), consistent cadence (3 posts/week minimum), original perspectives (not aggregation), network of credible engagers \\u2014 Anthropic employees, AI governance researchers in Brazil, Brazilian non-dev AI practitioners.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nContent Pillars\\\\t\\\\nPillar 1: Evidence\\\\tWeekly post anchored to primary source (KPMG, Workday, Anthropic, EU AI Act). Format: data point \\u2192 editorial reading \\u2192 operational implication.\\\\nPillar 2: Skill of Week\\\\tWalk through one Claude Skill from 24-skill corpus, with actual prompt/configuration and non-dev use case.\\\\nPillar 3: Case Study\\\\tReproducible workflow with before/after, time saved, evidence of error reduction. Review Journal voice.\\\\nPillar 4: Brazilian Regulatory\\\\tPL 2338, LGPD intersections with AI, EU AI Act Article 4 obligations for Brazilian firms with EU clients.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nCadence & Format\\\\t\\\\nWeekly Cadence\\\\t3 posts per week minimum. Mon: PT-BR operational tip (150-250 words). Wed: EN evidence-led analysis (250-400 words). Fri: bilingual case study or Brazilian regulatory. Two long-form LinkedIn articles per month.\\\\nDaily Engagement\\\\t30 minutes per day commenting substantively on 5-10 posts from people in target network.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nFirst 50 Followers Curation\\\\t\\\\nStrategy\\\\tEditorial team sees who engages with your posts. In days 1-30, deliberately curate first 50 connections: Anthropic employees publicly active on LinkedIn, AI governance researchers at Brazilian universities (USP, FGV, Insper), Brazilian non-dev AI creators with editorial sobriety, EU AI Act practitioners. Quality of network beats follower count for editorial visibility.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nKPI Targets\\\\t\\\\nDay 30\\\\t1,000 followers, 3 weekly posts maintained, average engagement rate >2%, 25 substantive comments delivered to target network per week.\\\\nDay 60\\\\t2,500 followers, 1 long-form article with >10k impressions, first inbound from ICP-002 unprompted.\\\\nDay 90\\\\t5,000 followers, average post >5k impressions, 3+ Anthropic-adjacent engagers commenting on posts, foundation visible for editorial invitation in months 4-9.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 10_AMBASSADOR_PATH\\\\n\\\\nComponent\\\\tContent\\\\nProgram Status\\\\tClaude Community Ambassadors Program is open as of March 2026 at claude.com/community/ambassadors. Applications reviewed on rolling basis. Program prioritizes geographic diversity and explicitly welcomes international applicants. No developer title requirement. Benefits include event funding, monthly API credits, private Slack with Anthropic team, Builders Council access, swag, and pre-release feature access. Program does not pay directly. Ambassadors cannot also represent company whose core business competes with Anthropic's.\\\\nLeonardo Fit Assessment\\\\tStrong fit on three dimensions, weak on one. Strong: editorial credibility (Review Journal, BBC+HBR+AP), operational depth (24 Claude Skills), geographic diversity (Brazil, S\\u00e3o Paulo, currently under-represented). Weak: community/event track record currently absent. D-A (S\\u00e3o Paulo Claude meetups) directly closes this gap. Without it, application leans on content credibility alone, which is not program's selection signal.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nAsset Checklist Before Application\\\\t\\\\n\\u2713 Repository\\\\tPublic repository live with v1.0.0 release tag\\\\n\\u2713 LinkedIn\\\\tProfile gold-standard with 3,000+ followers and consistent posting record (>30 days)\\\\n\\u2713 First Meetup\\\\tClaude meetup hosted in S\\u00e3o Paulo (online or hybrid acceptable) with public recap\\\\n\\u2713 Medium\\\\tAt least one Medium long-form (D2) published\\\\n\\u2713 GitHub Case\\\\tCase study (D3) published on GitHub\\\\n\\u2713 Claude Product\\\\tDocumented experience with Claude Code OR Claude Cowork \\u2014 publish at least one workflow case study using one of these two products\\\\n\\u2713 \"name\": \"bash_tool\", \"content\": [{\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"{\\\"returncode\\\":0,\\\"stdout\\\":\\\"\\u2713 Landing\\\\tBilingual README on aigovbrasil.com landing\\\\n\\u2713 Email\\\\tProfessional email leonardo@aigovbrasil.com (or equivalent)\\\\n\\u2713 Essay\\\\tApplication essay: why Brazil, why now, what community gap you close\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nDifferentiating Angle\\\\t\\\\nApplication Thesis\\\\tLead with under-represented operator angle: Brazil ranks #3 globally in raw Claude.ai usage (Anthropic Economic Index) but has minimal public Claude community infrastructure for non-developers. Application thesis: 'I am building the missing Brazilian Claude community for non-developer professionals \\u2014 bilingual, evidence-led, anchored to AI literacy obligations under EU AI Act Article 4 for Brazilian firms with EU exposure.' This is a thesis no other Brazilian applicant can credibly make.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nTiming\\\\tApply at day 75-90, not earlier. Earlier signals lack of substance; later misses rolling-review window. By day 75: 1 meetup hosted, 30+ LinkedIn posts, repository at v1.0+, one Medium piece \\u2014 minimum credible portfolio for non-pre-existing-influencer application.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 11_BEYOND_SCOPE\\\\n\\\\nID\\\\tTopic\\\\tInsight\\\\nB-01\\\\tClaude Cowork unique fit\\\\tAmbassador page lists 'meaningful experience with Claude Code or Claude Cowork' as requirement. Cowork is Anthropic's desktop tool for non-developers \\u2014 exactly Leonardo's ICP. Publishing Cowork workflow case studies positions Leonardo as Brazilian Cowork-fluent operator, niche almost no other applicant can claim.\\\\nB-02\\\\tAnchor to Brazilian regulation\\\\tPL 2338/2023 (Brazilian AI bill) and LGPD intersections with AI are under-covered in PT-BR content. Anchoring to BOTH EU AI Act Article 4 AND Brazilian PL 2338 is positioning moat \\u2014 most Brazilian AI creators ignore PL 2338 because not yet enacted.\\\\nB-03\\\\tFirst-50-followers curation\\\\tLinkedIn's editorial team for Top Voice reviews not just posts but who engages. Deliberately cultivating first 50 connections (Anthropic employees, AI governance researchers at USP/FGV/Insper, Brazilian non-dev creators with editorial sobriety) shapes what editorial team sees when evaluating in months 6-9.\\\\nB-04\\\\tBrazil Claude usage rank\\\\tPer project corpus (M-005, Anthropic Economic Index), Brazil ranks #3 globally in raw Claude.ai usage. Real underlying audience \\u2014 far larger than visible Brazilian Claude content community. Ambassador application thesis should lead with this gap: large user base, minimal Portuguese-language operator content.\\\\nB-05\\\\t1,000-free-subscriber threshold\\\\tPaid newsletter conversion below ~1,000 free subscribers produces noisy signal. If day-80 free count below 1,000, rational move is delay paid launch by two weeks rather than launch into weak validation window and conclude paid newsletter does not work.\\\\nB-06\\\\tEU AI Act Article 4 timeline\\\\tArticle 4 entered application Feb 2, 2025, but broader AI Act enforcement timeline staged through Aug 2025 (GPAI obligations) and Aug 2026 (high-risk system obligations begin). Content should be technically precise about which provisions in force when. Imprecision caught by EU-based readers damages editorial credibility.\\\\nB-07\\\\tINPI prior-art search\\\\tINPI has public search portal. Confirming 'AI Gov Brasil' (and 'AIGovBrasil' single token) has no Brazilian trademark collision takes 15 minutes. Doing this day 1 prevents discovering collision after public launch \\u2014 which would force rebrand.\\\\nB-08\\\\t'Not a lawyer' boundary\\\\tLeonardo is not lawyer. Content giving compliance guidance high-risk in two directions: legal liability if misapplied, brand damage if Brazilian or EU lawyers read as encroachment. 'Operational literacy' framing keeps on operator ground where Leonardo has actual authority.\\\\nB-09\\\\tCubo Ita\\u00fa as SP venue\\\\tCubo Ita\\u00fa in S\\u00e3o Paulo (Vila Ol\\u00edmpia) regularly hosts AI/tech meetups at no cost in exchange for community building. They have existing AI track. 30-day lead time and written meetup thesis usually enough to secure slot. Lowest-friction path to in-person meetup credibility.\\\\nB-10\\\\tTrust & Safety AUP\\\\tBuilding brand explicitly aligned with Anthropic while not respecting Anthropic's Usage Policies is fastest way to disqualify from ambassador consideration. Read current Anthropic Usage Policies and Acceptable Use Policy before publishing any post mentioning Claude capabilities, re-read quarterly. Non-obvious because most creators never check.\\\\nB-11\\\\tBuilders Council pre-application\\\\tAnthropic Builders Council is touchpoint ambassadors get access to, but also place where Anthropic engineers and researchers publicly visible (Discord, community forums, GitHub discussions). Becoming recognizable voice in those public surfaces before formally applying makes application recognition rather than introduction.\\\\nB-12\\\\tCase study highest-leverage\\\\tAcross every channel \\u2014 GitHub, LinkedIn, Medium, newsletter, ambassador application \\u2014 single artifact that travels furthest is reproducible case study with before/after, time saved, evidence of error reduction, Claude configuration another operator can copy. D3 is one. 90-day plan should produce at least three more. Volume of case studies closest proxy to operator authority.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 12_METRICS_TRACKER\\\\n\\\\nMetric\\\\tDay 30 Target\\\\tDay 60 Target\\\\tDay 90 Target\\\\tActual\\\\tStatus\\\\nLinkedIn Followers\\\\t1,000\\\\t2,500\\\\t5,000\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nAvg Post Impressions\\\\t500+\\\\t2,000+\\\\t5,000+\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nNewsletter Free Subs\\\\t100\\\\t500\\\\t1,000\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nNewsletter Paid Subs\\\\t\\u2014\\\\t\\u2014\\\\t30+\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nGitHub Stars\\\\t10\\\\t25\\\\t50\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nMeetups Hosted\\\\t1\\\\t2\\\\t3\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nMedium Articles\\\\t0-1\\\\t1-2\\\\t2-3\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nGitHub Case Studies\\\\t1\\\\t2\\\\t3\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nLinkedIn Posts Total\\\\t10+\\\\t25+\\\\t40+\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nAnthropic Engagers\\\\t1\\\\t2\\\\t3+\\\\t\\\\t\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 13_CHANGELOG\\\\n\\\\nDate\\\\tVersion\\\\tSession By\\\\tChanges\\\\n2026-05-14\\\\tv3.0.0\\\\tClaude Opus 4.7\\\\tInitial Traveller DB v3 \\u2014 AI Gov Brasil. Converted JSON strategic plan into executive Excel workbook. 14 sheets: CMD, Strategic Read, Brand Identity, Positioning, ICP, Decisions, Roadmap, Risks, LinkedIn Strategy, Ambassador Path, Beyond Scope, Metrics Tracker, Changelog, Next 24H Action.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 14_NEXT_24H_ACTION\\\\n\\\\nNEXT 24H ACTION\\\\n\\\\nTask: INPI Trademark Prior-Art Search\\\\n\\\\nAction\\\\nOpen the INPI trademark portal (busca.inpi.gov.br) and conduct a prior-art search for 'AI Gov Brasil' and 'AIGovBrasil' as nominative marks across classes 9, 35, 41, and 42. Document the result in a single-page memo.\\\\n\\\\nRationale\\\\nThis is the only action that, if skipped, can force a full rebrand after launch \\u2014 making it the highest-asymmetric-risk task in the entire 90-day plan.\\\\n\\\\nEstimated Time\\\\n30 minutes\\\\n\\\\nConfidence\\\\nHIGH\\\\n\\\\nClasses to Search\\\\n\\u2022 Class 9: Computer software, electronic publications \\u2022 Class 35: Business management consulting \\u2022 Class 41: Education services, training \\u2022 Class 42: Software as a service, technological consulting\\\",\\\"stderr\\\":\\\"\\\"}\", \"uuid\": \"3b1e9fe6-2ee4-401b-89b1-5466dd5831ad\"}], \"is_error\": false, \"structured_content\": null, \"meta\": null, \"message\": null, \"integration_name\": null, \"mcp_server_url\": null, \"integration_icon_url\": null, \"icon_name\": \"commandLine\", \"display_content\": {\"type\": \"json_block\", \"json_block\": \"{\\\"returncode\\\":0,\\\"stdout\\\":\\\"\\u2713 Landing\\\\tBilingual README on aigovbrasil.com landing\\\\n\\u2713 Email\\\\tProfessional email leonardo@aigovbrasil.com (or equivalent)\\\\n\\u2713 Essay\\\\tApplication essay: why Brazil, why now, what community gap you close\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nDifferentiating Angle\\\\t\\\\nApplication Thesis\\\\tLead with under-represented operator angle: Brazil ranks #3 globally in raw Claude.ai usage (Anthropic Economic Index) but has minimal public Claude community infrastructure for non-developers. Application thesis: 'I am building the missing Brazilian Claude community for non-developer professionals \\u2014 bilingual, evidence-led, anchored to AI literacy obligations under EU AI Act Article 4 for Brazilian firms with EU exposure.' This is a thesis no other Brazilian applicant can credibly make.\\\\n\\\\t\\\\nTiming\\\\tApply at day 75-90, not earlier. Earlier signals lack of substance; later misses rolling-review window. By day 75: 1 meetup hosted, 30+ LinkedIn posts, repository at v1.0+, one Medium piece \\u2014 minimum credible portfolio for non-pre-existing-influencer application.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 11_BEYOND_SCOPE\\\\n\\\\nID\\\\tTopic\\\\tInsight\\\\nB-01\\\\tClaude Cowork unique fit\\\\tAmbassador page lists 'meaningful experience with Claude Code or Claude Cowork' as requirement. Cowork is Anthropic's desktop tool for non-developers \\u2014 exactly Leonardo's ICP. Publishing Cowork workflow case studies positions Leonardo as Brazilian Cowork-fluent operator, niche almost no other applicant can claim.\\\\nB-02\\\\tAnchor to Brazilian regulation\\\\tPL 2338/2023 (Brazilian AI bill) and LGPD intersections with AI are under-covered in PT-BR content. Anchoring to BOTH EU AI Act Article 4 AND Brazilian PL 2338 is positioning moat \\u2014 most Brazilian AI creators ignore PL 2338 because not yet enacted.\\\\nB-03\\\\tFirst-50-followers curation\\\\tLinkedIn's editorial team for Top Voice reviews not just posts but who engages. Deliberately cultivating first 50 connections (Anthropic employees, AI governance researchers at USP/FGV/Insper, Brazilian non-dev creators with editorial sobriety) shapes what editorial team sees when evaluating in months 6-9.\\\\nB-04\\\\tBrazil Claude usage rank\\\\tPer project corpus (M-005, Anthropic Economic Index), Brazil ranks #3 globally in raw Claude.ai usage. Real underlying audience \\u2014 far larger than visible Brazilian Claude content community. Ambassador application thesis should lead with this gap: large user base, minimal Portuguese-language operator content.\\\\nB-05\\\\t1,000-free-subscriber threshold\\\\tPaid newsletter conversion below ~1,000 free subscribers produces noisy signal. If day-80 free count below 1,000, rational move is delay paid launch by two weeks rather than launch into weak validation window and conclude paid newsletter does not work.\\\\nB-06\\\\tEU AI Act Article 4 timeline\\\\tArticle 4 entered application Feb 2, 2025, but broader AI Act enforcement timeline staged through Aug 2025 (GPAI obligations) and Aug 2026 (high-risk system obligations begin). Content should be technically precise about which provisions in force when. Imprecision caught by EU-based readers damages editorial credibility.\\\\nB-07\\\\tINPI prior-art search\\\\tINPI has public search portal. Confirming 'AI Gov Brasil' (and 'AIGovBrasil' single token) has no Brazilian trademark collision takes 15 minutes. Doing this day 1 prevents discovering collision after public launch \\u2014 which would force rebrand.\\\\nB-08\\\\t'Not a lawyer' boundary\\\\tLeonardo is not lawyer. Content giving compliance guidance high-risk in two directions: legal liability if misapplied, brand damage if Brazilian or EU lawyers read as encroachment. 'Operational literacy' framing keeps on operator ground where Leonardo has actual authority.\\\\nB-09\\\\tCubo Ita\\u00fa as SP venue\\\\tCubo Ita\\u00fa in S\\u00e3o Paulo (Vila Ol\\u00edmpia) regularly hosts AI/tech meetups at no cost in exchange for community building. They have existing AI track. 30-day lead time and written meetup thesis usually enough to secure slot. Lowest-friction path to in-person meetup credibility.\\\\nB-10\\\\tTrust & Safety AUP\\\\tBuilding brand explicitly aligned with Anthropic while not respecting Anthropic's Usage Policies is fastest way to disqualify from ambassador consideration. Read current Anthropic Usage Policies and Acceptable Use Policy before publishing any post mentioning Claude capabilities, re-read quarterly. Non-obvious because most creators never check.\\\\nB-11\\\\tBuilders Council pre-application\\\\tAnthropic Builders Council is touchpoint ambassadors get access to, but also place where Anthropic engineers and researchers publicly visible (Discord, community forums, GitHub discussions). Becoming recognizable voice in those public surfaces before formally applying makes application recognition rather than introduction.\\\\nB-12\\\\tCase study highest-leverage\\\\tAcross every channel \\u2014 GitHub, LinkedIn, Medium, newsletter, ambassador application \\u2014 single artifact that travels furthest is reproducible case study with before/after, time saved, evidence of error reduction, Claude configuration another operator can copy. D3 is one. 90-day plan should produce at least three more. Volume of case studies closest proxy to operator authority.\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 12_METRICS_TRACKER\\\\n\\\\nMetric\\\\tDay 30 Target\\\\tDay 60 Target\\\\tDay 90 Target\\\\tActual\\\\tStatus\\\\nLinkedIn Followers\\\\t1,000\\\\t2,500\\\\t5,000\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nAvg Post Impressions\\\\t500+\\\\t2,000+\\\\t5,000+\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nNewsletter Free Subs\\\\t100\\\\t500\\\\t1,000\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nNewsletter Paid Subs\\\\t\\u2014\\\\t\\u2014\\\\t30+\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nGitHub Stars\\\\t10\\\\t25\\\\t50\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nMeetups Hosted\\\\t1\\\\t2\\\\t3\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nMedium Articles\\\\t0-1\\\\t1-2\\\\t2-3\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nGitHub Case Studies\\\\t1\\\\t2\\\\t3\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nLinkedIn Posts Total\\\\t10+\\\\t25+\\\\t40+\\\\t\\\\t\\\\nAnthropic Engagers\\\\t1\\\\t2\\\\t3+\\\\t\\\\t\\\\n\\\\n## Sheet: 13_CHANGELOG\\\\n\\\\nDate\\\\tVersion\\\\tSession By\\\\tChanges\\\\n2026-05-14\\\\tv3.0.0\\\\tClaude Opus 4.7\\\\tInitial Traveller DB v3 \\u2014 AI Gov Brasil. \\\\n \\u00cdndice Proposto\\\\n\\\\n 05 Metodologia do \\u00cdndice\\\\n\\\\n 06 Aplica\\u00e7\\u00e3o ao Claude\\\\n\\\\n 07 Tabela Comparativa\\\\n\\\\n 08 Limita\\u00e7\\u00f5es & Quest\\u00f5es\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Aplica\\u00e7\\u00f5es Pr\\u00e1ticas\\\\n\\\\n 09 Recomenda\\u00e7\\u00f5es por P\\u00fablico\\\\n\\\\n 10 Checklist de Conformidade\\\\n\\\\n 11 Toolkit de Avalia\\u00e7\\u00e3o\\\\n\\\\n 12 Roadmap de Implementa\\u00e7\\u00e3o\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Recursos\\\\n\\\\n 13 Refer\\u00eancias & Documenta\\u00e7\\u00e3o\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n[H2] Sum\\u00e1rio Executivo\\\\n Maio 2026\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Fundamentos\\\\n \\\\n[H3] Sum\\u00e1rio Executivo\\\\n A no\\u00e7\\u00e3o de AI literacy nas fontes mais normativas n\\u00e3o \\u00e9 \\\\\\\"saber programar IA\\\\\\\", mas possuir compet\\u00eancias, conhecimentos e compreens\\u00e3o suficientes para usar, supervisionar e interpretar sistemas de IA de modo informado, consciente de oportunidades, riscos e poss\\u00edveis danos.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83c\\udfaf Defini\\u00e7\\u00e3o Legal\\\\n AI Act (Artigo 4): Define \\\\\\\"literacia no dom\\u00ednio da IA\\\\\\\" como compet\\u00eancias, conhecimentos e compreens\\u00e3o que permitam implanta\\u00e7\\u00e3o informada e consci\\u00eancia sobre oportunidades, riscos e poss\\u00edveis danos. Obriga prestadores e deployers a adotarem medidas proporcionais ao contexto, ao risco, ao perfil das pessoas envolvidas e aos grupos afetados.\\\\n\\\\n Vig\\u00eancia: Disposi\\u00e7\\u00f5es gerais aplicam-se desde 2 de fevereiro de 2025; supervis\\u00e3o por autoridades nacionais come\\u00e7a em agosto de 2026.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83c\\udf0d Converg\\u00eancia com Marcos Educacionais\\\\n Organiza\\u00e7\\u00e3oFocoDimens\\u00f5es Centrais\\\\n UNESCOEstudantes & Professores12-15 compet\\u00eancias em 4-5 dimens\\u00f5es; progress\\u00e3o por n\\u00edveis\\\\n OECDEduca\\u00e7\\u00e3o & TrabalhoEngajamento cr\\u00edtico; letramento geral, n\\u00e3o especializa\\u00e7\\u00e3o\\\\n AI ActConformidade Regulat\\u00f3riaCompreens\\u00e3o conceitual, uso pr\\u00e1tico, avalia\\u00e7\\u00e3o cr\\u00edtica, \\u00e9tica/governan\\u00e7a\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83d\\udcca Maturidade da Mensura\\u00e7\\u00e3o\\\\n Aviso Metodol\\u00f3gico: A revis\\u00e3o sistem\\u00e1tica identificou 16 escalas validadas em 22 estudos, majoritariamente de autorrelato; apenas 3 eram baseadas em desempenho, e praticamente nenhuma tinha validade transcultural testada. Um estudo de 2026 mostrou baixa correla\\u00e7\\u00e3o entre medidas autorrelatadas e objetivas.\\\\n\\\\n Implica\\u00e7\\u00e3o: Qualquer \\u00edndice s\\u00e9rio para LLMs deve ser multim\\u00e9todo: checklist documental, testes comportamentais, medidas de calibra\\u00e7\\u00e3o, evid\\u00eancias p\\u00fablicas de seguran\\u00e7a e governan\\u00e7a.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83c\\udfc6 Resultado do \\u00cdndice Proposto\\\\n \\\\n Claude (Fam\\u00edlia 4.x)\\\\nAplica\\u00e7\\u00e3o hipot\\u00e9tica do AI Literacy Index\\\\n\\\\n 81/100\\\\n\\\\n ALTA\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n Interpreta\\u00e7\\u00e3o: Documenta\\u00e7\\u00e3o e governan\\u00e7a p\\u00fablicas acima da m\\u00e9dia, boa infraestrutura para supervis\\u00e3o e uso cr\\u00edtico, mas com lacunas importantes em calibra\\u00e7\\u00e3o p\\u00fablica padronizada, fidelidade de cita\\u00e7\\u00f5es e comparabilidade externa entre benchmarks.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Fundamentos\\\\n \\\\n[H3] AI Act & Base Regulat\\u00f3ria\\\\n O AI Act estabelece obriga\\u00e7\\u00f5es legais espec\\u00edficas para literacia em IA, com defini\\u00e7\\u00f5es precisas e cronograma de implementa\\u00e7\\u00e3o j\\u00e1 iniciado.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\u2696\\ufe0f Defini\\u00e7\\u00e3o Legal (Artigo 4)\\\\n \\\\\\\"Literacia no dom\\u00ednio da IA\\\\\\\" = compet\\u00eancias, conhecimentos e compreens\\u00e3o que permitam implanta\\u00e7\\u00e3o informada e consci\\u00eancia sobre oportunidades, riscos e poss\\u00edveis danos.\\u2014 AI Act, Artigo 4 (vers\\u00e3o em portugu\\u00eas)\\\\n\\\\n Obriga\\u00e7\\u00e3o: Prestadores e deployers devem adotar medidas \\\\\\\"na medida do poss\\u00edvel\\\\\\\" para garantir n\\u00edvel suficiente de literacia, considerando: conhecimento t\\u00e9cnico, experi\\u00eancia, forma\\u00e7\\u00e3o, contexto de uso e pessoas afetadas.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83d\\udcc5 Cronograma de Aplica\\u00e7\\u00e3o\\\\n \\\\n 02 FEV 2025\\\\nDisposi\\u00e7\\u00f5es Gerais Entram em Vigor\\\\nArtigo 4 (literacia em IA) passa a ser obrigat\\u00f3rio para prestadores e deployers\\\\n\\\\n AGO \\\\n\\\\n Contexto Estrat\\u00e9gico\\\\n\\\\n Por que isso importa agora\\\\n\\\\n O problema, o custo da ina\\u00e7\\u00e3o e os fatores de mudan\\u00e7a\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n \\\\n O Problema Estrat\\u00e9gico\\\\n\\\\n A ado\\u00e7\\u00e3o de IA generativa acelerou radicalmente no trabalho. Profissionais usam IA para pesquisa, an\\u00e1lise, escrita, c\\u00f3digo e decis\\u00e3o. Mas acesso n\\u00e3o \\u00e9 maturidade.\\\\n\\\\n A maior parte do uso ainda \\u00e9 baseada em prompts isolados, sem personaliza\\u00e7\\u00e3o, sem verifica\\u00e7\\u00e3o e sem governan\\u00e7a. O resultado: retrabalho elevado, confian\\u00e7a falsa em outputs polidos mas imprecisos, e exposi\\u00e7\\u00e3o a riscos de compliance, privacidade e reputa\\u00e7\\u00e3o.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n O Custo da Ina\\u00e7\\u00e3o\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n Produtividade\\\\n\\\\n Tempo gasto corrigindo outputs mal especificados ou verificando informa\\u00e7\\u00f5es alucinadas.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Qualidade\\\\n\\\\n Decis\\u00f5es baseadas em respostas gen\\u00e9ricas ou desatualizadas.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Governan\\u00e7a\\\\n\\\\n Exposi\\u00e7\\u00e3o de dados sens\\u00edveis, falta de trilha de auditoria, uso sem supervis\\u00e3o.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Competitividade\\\\n\\\\n Organiza\\u00e7\\u00f5es que n\\u00e3o estruturam uso de IA ficam mais lentas e menos confi\\u00e1veis.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n Mudan\\u00e7a de Contexto \\u2014 Por Que Agora\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n Regulat\\u00f3rio\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n O AI Act da UE torna AI literacy uma obriga\\u00e7\\u00e3o legal desde fevereiro de 2025. O Artigo 4 exige forma\\u00e7\\u00e3o proporcional ao contexto, risco e pessoas afetadas.\\\\n \\u2197 AI Act, Artigo 4\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n Comportamental\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n O AI Fluency Index analisou 9.830 conversas e identificou que 85,7% tinham itera\\u00e7\\u00e3o. Conversas iterativas questionam mais o racioc\\u00ednio e identificam melhor o contexto ausente.\\\\n \\u2197 Anthropic AI Fluency Index\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n Operacional\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Personaliza\\u00e7\\u00e3o em camadas transforma IA de ferramenta gen\\u00e9rica em sistema alinhado ao trabalho real. A maioria n\\u00e3o separa contexto, m\\u00e9todo e output corretamente.\\\\n \\u2197 Documenta\\u00e7\\u00e3o oficial Claude\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n Estrat\\u00e9gico\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Acelerar aprendizado exige agenda estruturada, perguntas sobre o passado e testes de hip\\u00f3teses \\u2014 n\\u00e3o a\\u00e7\\u00e3o imediata sem contexto.\\\\n \\u2197 M\\u00e9todo Watkins \\u2014 First 90 Days\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n 02\\\\n\\\\n A-Z da AI Literacy & AI Fluency\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n Base Metodol\\u00f3gica\\\\n\\\\n Fontes e base metodol\\u00f3gica\\\\n\\\\n Fontes prim\\u00e1rias, regula\\u00e7\\u00e3o oficial, pesquisa comportamental e documenta\\u00e7\\u00e3o t\\u00e9cnica\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Este guia foi desenvolvido com base em fontes prim\\u00e1rias. Executivo\\\\n A no\\u00e7\\u00e3o de AI literacy nas fontes mais normativas n\\u00e3o \\u00e9 \\\\\\\"saber programar IA\\\\\\\", mas possuir compet\\u00eancias, conhecimentos e compreens\\u00e3o suficientes para usar, supervisionar e interpretar sistemas de IA de modo informado, consciente de oportunidades, riscos e poss\\u00edveis danos.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83c\\udfaf Defini\\u00e7\\u00e3o Legal\\\\n AI Act (Artigo 4): Define \\\\\\\"literacia no dom\\u00ednio da IA\\\\\\\" como compet\\u00eancias, conhecimentos e compreens\\u00e3o que permitam implanta\\u00e7\\u00e3o informada e consci\\u00eancia sobre oportunidades, riscos e poss\\u00edveis danos. Obriga prestadores e deployers a adotarem medidas proporcionais ao contexto, ao risco, ao perfil das pessoas envolvidas e aos grupos afetados.\\\\n\\\\n Vig\\u00eancia: Disposi\\u00e7\\u00f5es gerais aplicam-se desde 2 de fevereiro de 2025; supervis\\u00e3o por autoridades nacionais come\\u00e7a em agosto de 2026.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83c\\udf0d Converg\\u00eancia com Marcos Educacionais\\\\n Organiza\\u00e7\\u00e3oFocoDimens\\u00f5es Centrais\\\\n UNESCOEstudantes & Professores12-15 compet\\u00eancias em 4-5 dimens\\u00f5es; progress\\u00e3o por n\\u00edveis\\\\n OECDEduca\\u00e7\\u00e3o & TrabalhoEngajamento cr\\u00edtico; letramento geral, n\\u00e3o especializa\\u00e7\\u00e3o\\\\n AI ActConformidade Regulat\\u00f3riaCompreens\\u00e3o conceitual, uso pr\\u00e1tico, avalia\\u00e7\\u00e3o cr\\u00edtica, \\u00e9tica/governan\\u00e7a\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83d\\udcca Maturidade da Mensura\\u00e7\\u00e3o\\\\n Aviso Metodol\\u00f3gico: A revis\\u00e3o sistem\\u00e1tica identificou 16 escalas validadas em 22 estudos, majoritariamente de autorrelato; apenas 3 eram baseadas em desempenho, e praticamente nenhuma tinha validade transcultural testada. Um estudo de 2026 mostrou baixa correla\\u00e7\\u00e3o entre medidas autorrelatadas e objetivas.\\\\n\\\\n Implica\\u00e7\\u00e3o: Qualquer \\u00edndice s\\u00e9rio para LLMs deve ser multim\\u00e9todo: checklist documental, testes comportamentais, medidas de calibra\\u00e7\\u00e3o, evid\\u00eancias p\\u00fablicas de seguran\\u00e7a e governan\\u00e7a.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83c\\udfc6 Resultado do \\u00cdndice Proposto\\\\n \\\\n Claude (Fam\\u00edlia 4.x)\\\\nAplica\\u00e7\\u00e3o hipot\\u00e9tica do AI Literacy Index\\\\n\\\\n 81/100\\\\n\\\\n ALTA\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n Interpreta\\u00e7\\u00e3o: Documenta\\u00e7\\u00e3o e governan\\u00e7a p\\u00fablicas acima da m\\u00e9dia, boa infraestrutura para supervis\\u00e3o e uso cr\\u00edtico, mas com lacunas importantes em calibra\\u00e7\\u00e3o p\\u00fablica padronizada, fidelidade de cita\\u00e7\\u00f5es e comparabilidade externa entre benchmarks.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n Fundamentos\\\\n \\\\n[H3] AI Act & Base Regulat\\u00f3ria\\\\n O AI Act estabelece obriga\\u00e7\\u00f5es legais espec\\u00edficas para literacia em IA, com defini\\u00e7\\u00f5es precisas e cronograma de implementa\\u00e7\\u00e3o j\\u00e1 iniciado.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\u2696\\ufe0f Defini\\u00e7\\u00e3o Legal (Artigo 4)\\\\n \\\\\\\"Literacia no dom\\u00ednio da IA\\\\\\\" = compet\\u00eancias, conhecimentos e compreens\\u00e3o que permitam implanta\\u00e7\\u00e3o informada e consci\\u00eancia sobre oportunidades, riscos e poss\\u00edveis danos.\\u2014 AI Act, Artigo 4 (vers\\u00e3o em portugu\\u00eas)\\\\n\\\\n Obriga\\u00e7\\u00e3o: Prestadores e deployers devem adotar medidas \\\\\\\"na medida do poss\\u00edvel\\\\\\\" para garantir n\\u00edvel suficiente de literacia, considerando: conhecimento t\\u00e9cnico, experi\\u00eancia, forma\\u00e7\\u00e3o, contexto de uso e pessoas afetadas.\\\\n\\\\n \\\\n\\\\n \\\\n \\\\n[H4] \\ud83d\\udcc5 Cronograma de This is the spreadsheet Phase 4 must fill | Foundational |\\n| 3 | `ai-literacy-governance-ebook.html` | Synthesis ebook | **Regulatory + measurement evidence** \\u2014 EU AI Act Article 4, UNESCO/OECD frameworks, AI Literacy Index methodology, systematic review of 16 validated scales | Primary evidence |\\n| 4 | `az-ai-literacy-ebook.html` | Synthesis ebook | **Behavioral + operational evidence** \\u2014 Anthropic AI Fluency Index (9,830 conversations, 85.7% iteration), 4D framework, layered personalization argument | Primary evidence |\\n| 5 | `aigovbrasil-skill-library-executive.numbers` | Skill library navigator | **Operational artifact catalog** \\u2014 24+ skills in canonical 4-layer format (First Principles + ICP / Problem Tree / 5W2H / Evidence + CMD + WOW), plus PASTEL governance context and glossary | Internal evidence |\\n| 6 | `aigovbrasil_v3_exec.xlsx` | Strategic executive plan | **Project context** \\u2014 14 sheets covering CMD, positioning, ICP, 90-day roadmap, risk register R-001 to R-008, LinkedIn strategy, Ambassador path. Contains M-007 to M-013 verified evidence stack | Contextual + primary citations |\\n| 7 | `empower-v4-ai-usage-evaluator_2.skill` | Skill package (SKILL.md + 894-line Python) | **Measurement instrument** \\u2014 maturity formula (6 weighted components \\u2192 L1\\u2013L5 levels), three-baseline lift methodology, prompt-pattern risk clusters | Methodological |\\n| 8 | `bussola-unified.skill` | Skill package (26 KB SKILL.md) | **Deterministic-framework exemplar** \\u2014 9-phase consultative method with hardcoded gates G0\\u2013G6 and 28 artifacts in 3 tracks; concrete instance of the \\\"FD\\\" component | Methodological |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Thesis component coverage \\u2014 where each variable in the formula has support\\n\\nThe thesis formula `L + P + S + FD + C \\u2192 \\u2191 performance, \\u2193 rework` maps against the file corpus as follows. The asymmetry of coverage is the first auditor-grade finding: every input variable has source material, but the outcome side (productivity, rework reduction) has weak primary evidence and depends almost entirely on inference.\\n\\n```\\nTHESIS COMPONENT COVERAGE\\n\\u2502\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 L (AI literacy)\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 Direct evidence  \\u2500\\u2500 ai-literacy-governance-ebook.html (AI Act Art. 4, UNESCO, OECD)\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 Direct evidence  \\u2500\\u2500 az-ai-literacy-ebook.html (Anthropic AI Fluency Index)\\n\\u2502 aigovbrasil-skill-library-executive.numbers (24 skills as personalization layer)\\n\\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 Gap              \\u2500\\u2500 no primary peer-reviewed study quantifying P\\u2192outcome\\n\\u2502\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 S (SKILL.md / reusable skills)\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 Official docs    \\u2500\\u2500 ai_research_framework_template.xlsx \\u2192 S3 Anthropic docs\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 Operational      \\u2500\\u2500 bussola-unified.skill + 24 catalog entries (.numbers)\\n\\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 Gap              \\u2500\\u2500
